name: ScaleFactor
tagline: AI-automated bookkeeping and financial management for SMBs
description: ScaleFactor raised $100M+ claiming AI-powered accounting, but a Forbes investigation revealed most work was done by human bookkeepers outsourced to the Philippines.
story: ScaleFactor raised over $100 million from Bessemer Venture Partners and Coatue Management, marketing itself as an AI-powered accounting platform for small businesses. The pitch was compelling: automated bookkeeping that eliminated the need for human accountants. A Forbes investigation told a different story. Most of ScaleFactor's work was done by human bookkeepers outsourced to the Philippines, not AI. Customers reported frequent errors in financial statements. The company publicly blamed COVID-19 for its June 2020 shutdown, but internal mismanagement and the gap between AI promises and human-powered reality were the actual causes. ScaleFactor became one of the earliest high-profile examples of 'AI washing' in fintech.
